message.content:
Adversarial refuter: ASSUME this finding is FALSE; disprove it from the code at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay. survives=true only if you genuinely cannot.
P7-O-1: CV-O-1's strict decode silently REVERTS scrubbing for any transcript with one invalid UTF-8 byte — re-leaking the exact secrets/PII it was meant to redact
scripts/capture_transcripts.py:108-113
CV-O-1 replaced the per-suffix gate with a decode-strict classifier: `try: text = raw.decode("utf-8")` then scrub, `except UnicodeDecodeError: data = raw` (copy verbatim). This introduces a follow-on regression of CV-O-1's own goal and of every prior scrubbing fix (#F-024/#F-051/#G-007/#H-018). PRE-fix, .jsonl files were decoded with `errors="replace"`, so they were ALWAYS scrubbed regardless of any malformed byte — only the single invalid byte became U+FFFD, the rest of the file (and all secrets/PII in it) was still redacted. POST-fix, a STRICT decode means a single invalid UTF-8 byte anywhere in a .jsonl/.json transcript raises UnicodeDecodeError and routes the WHOLE file to the verbatim branch, copying it byte-for-byte with NO scrubbing. Claude tool-result transcripts routinely embed arbitrary subprocess stdout (hexdumps, non-UTF-8/latin-1 file reads, truncated multibyte from a killed process) inside the JSONL string values, so this is a common, not exotic, condition. The net effect: the highest-severity privacy guarantee (no operator email/username/path + no credential-shaped secret in committed transcripts) now fails open precisely for the files most likely to contain raw dumped data. This is the #H-001 -> #CV-M-1 class the audit targets: the new code is the defect.
PoC: import importlib.util, pathlib, tempfile
spec=importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('cap','scripts/capture_transcripts.py')
cap=importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(cap)
src=pathlib.Path(tempfile.mkdtemp())/'sess.jsonl'
# realistic: a tool-result string containing a secret + PII + one latin-1 byte (0x80) from subprocess output
src.write_bytes(b'{"out":"[REDACTED] caf\xe9byte=\x80 /Users/[REDACTED]/x"}')
raw=src.read_bytes()
try: text=raw.decode('utf-8'); data=cap._scrub(text).encode('utf-8'); branch='scrub'
except UnicodeDecodeError: data=raw; branch='verbatim'
print(branch)                       # -> verbatim
print(b'[REDACTED]' in data)      # -> True  (secret LEAKED)
print(b'[REDACTED]' in data)       # -> True  (username LEAKED)
print(b'REDACTED' in data)          # -> False (nothing scrubbed)
# Under the pre-CV-O-1 code this same .jsonl was decoded with errors='replace' and fully scrubbed.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

80	    for pat in _SECRET_PATTERNS:
81	        if pat.groups == 2:
82	            text = pat.sub(r"\1[REDACTED]\2", text)
83	        elif pat.groups == 1:
84	            text = pat.sub(r"\1[REDACTED]", text)
85	        else:
86	            text = pat.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
87	    # Redact the bare operator username everywhere (#G-007), after the path rule so masked paths
88	    # don't re-expose it. Done last so it also catches it inside project-dir slugs / git authors.
89	    if _USERNAME_RE is not None:
90	        text = _USERNAME_RE.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
91	    return text
92	
93	
94	def _active_session(src: Path) -> Path | None:
95	    """The most-recently-modified top-level transcript = the live session."""
96	    candidates = sorted(src.glob("*.jsonl"), key=lambda p: p.stat().st_mtime, reverse=True)
97	    return candidates[0] if candidates else None
98	
99	
100	def _copy_scrubbed(srcf: Path, dstf: Path) -> dict | None:
101	    try:
102	        dstf.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
103	        raw = srcf.read_bytes()
104	        # Scrub EVERY text file, not only .jsonl (#CV-O-1): the session subtree also holds .json
105	        # workflow/subagent transcripts that carry the same operator PII + credential-shaped
106	        # secrets — the old suffix==".jsonl" gate copied those RAW, bypassing redaction entirely.
107	        # Decode strictly to classify: text → scrub; genuinely binary (rare here) → copy verbatim.
108	        try:
109	            text = raw.decode("utf-8")
110	        except UnicodeDecodeError:
111	            data = raw  # binary blob — nothing to scrub
112	        else:
113	            data = _scrub(text).encode("utf-8")
114	        if dstf.exists() and dstf.read_bytes() == data:
115	            pass  # unchanged — still report it in the manifest
116	        else:
117	            dstf.write_bytes(data)
118	        return {
119	            "path": str(dstf.relative_to(DEST.parent.parent)),
120	            "bytes": len(data),
121	            "sha256": hashlib.sha256(data).hexdigest(),
122	        }
123	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — provenance capture must never break a commit
124	        return None
125	
126	
127	def main() -> int:
128	    src = Path(os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRANSCRIPT_SRC", str(DEFAULT_SRC)))
129	    if not src.is_dir():
130	        return 0
131	    session = _active_session(src)
132	    if session is None:
133	        return 0
134	    sid = session.stem
135	    captured: list[dict] = []
136	
137	    rec = _copy_scrubbed(session, DEST / f"{sid}.jsonl")
138	    if rec:
139	        captured.append(rec)

content.text: The code at lines 108-113 exactly matches what the finding describes. Strict UTF-8 decode, with the `except UnicodeDecodeError` branch copying raw bytes verbatim with no scrubbing. Let me verify the claim with the PoC and confirm the pre-fix behavior was `errors="replace"`.

content.text:
Confirmed. The finding is fully substantiated:

- POST-fix (current code, lines 108-113): strict decode → one invalid byte (0x80) routes the whole file to verbatim copy → secret and username both leaked, nothing redacted.
- PRE-fix (`errors="replace"`, confirmed in git diff at e3eb708): same bytes get fully scrubbed → secret and username redacted.

The attempt to disprove failed at every step. The code matches the cited lines exactly, the PoC reproduces, git history confirms the `errors="replace"` → strict decode regression, and `_scrub` demonstrably would have caught the secret/PII under the old path. The regression is real and is genuinely caused by CV-O-1's own change.

input.reasoning: CONFIRMED — could not disprove. Code at scripts/capture_transcripts.py:108-113 matches the finding verbatim: `try: text = raw.decode("utf-8") ... except UnicodeDecodeError: data = raw` (raw copied with NO scrubbing). The PoC reproduces exactly: a .jsonl byte string containing `[REDACTED]...`, `caf\xe9`, a single invalid latin-1 byte `\x80`, and `/Users/[REDACTED]/x` raises UnicodeDecodeError under strict decode, routing the entire file to the verbatim branch → branch=verbatim, secret leaked=True, username leaked=True, REDACTED present=False. Git history confirms the regression is CV-O-1's own change: commit e3eb708 (hardening-p6, "CV-O-1") replaced `data = _scrub(raw.decode("utf-8", errors="replace")).encode("utf-8")` with the strict `decode("utf-8")` + verbatim-on-error pattern. I directly verified the PRE-fix `errors="replace"` path on the identical bytes: secret leaked=False, username leaked=False, REDACTED present=True, and _USERNAME_RE is active — i.e., the old code fully scrubbed exactly what the new code leaks. The defect is fail-open precisely for transcripts most likely to embed non-UTF-8 subprocess stdout (hexdumps, latin-1 file reads, truncated multibyte), which Claude tool-result JSONL routinely carries. While CV-O-1 correctly broadened scrubbing to .json files (a genuine improvement), it simultaneously swapped lossy decoding for strict decoding, re-leaking secrets/PII (#F-024/#F-051/#G-007/#H-018 class) for any transcript containing a single invalid UTF-8 byte. Fix: restore `errors="replace"` (or scrub the latin-1/lossy-decoded text) instead of copying raw bytes verbatim. File: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/scripts/capture_transcripts.py:108-113.

content.text: Confirmed the finding survives. The regression at `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/scripts/capture_transcripts.py:108-113` is real and reproducible — strict UTF-8 decode silently reverts all scrubbing for any transcript containing a single invalid byte, re-leaking secrets and PII that the pre-fix `errors="replace"` path correctly redacted.
